stories.title: EU to tell countries to fight poverty — without offering fresh cash
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission will on Wednesday ask EU countries to eradicate poverty, arguing that governments can access the money to act if they use existing EU funds more effectively. The plan, set out in the Commission’s first anti-poverty strategy and seen by POLITICO, will urge governments to better coordinate national policies, engage young people, support those living paycheck to paycheck and review protections for older and other vulnerable people. But for now the strategy comes w

stories.title: Top official to leave EU’s diplomatic service
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — One of the top officials in the European External Action Service is set to leave her post after just over a year in office. Belén Martínez Carbonell is to stand down as secretary general of the EEAS, according to eight diplomats and officials with knowledge of the plans, all granted anonymity to speak freely. Two of them said Carbonell was already sharing the news with colleagues. No timeline has been given for her departure. The Spanish official, who has worked in the EEAS since 2010

stories.title: Trump’s World Liberty Financial venture sues crypto entrepreneur for defamation
stories.summary: Suit alleges that Hong Kong-based Justin Sun engaged in a campaign to ‘torch’ the company’s reputation Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email World Liberty Financial, the crypto venture co-founded by Donald Trump and his sons, said on Monday it had filed a defamation lawsuit in Florida state court against the Hong Kong -based crypto entrepreneur Justin Sun, as a dispute escalates between the project and one of its most prominent backers. World Liberty posted a copy of its lawsuit on X

stories.title: Central Europe is a laboratory for political trends | Letters
stories.summary: Dr Sean Hanley responds to an editorial on the collapse of social democratic parties as rightwing nationalism flourishes Your editorial on the politics of central Europe ( 28 April ) rightly notes the collapse of many traditional centre-left parties. But its explanation is incomplete and oddly exceptionalist. Much of what you describe – the erosion of social democratic parties after market liberalisation, the political aftershocks of the financial crisis, migration-driven cultural conflict and t

stories.title: The Guardian view on Trump, Merz and Europe’s security: EU countries cannot go it alone | Editorial
stories.summary: The announcement of the withdrawal of thousands of US troops from Germany underlines the urgency of a pan-European defence strategy As Donald Trump’s second term has become overshadowed by plunging poll ratings and an illegal, ill-advised war in the Middle East, European governments have regularly been singled out to bear the brunt of the US president’s growing frustration. Sir Keir Starmer’s refusal to militarily back the attack on Iran led to unfavourable comparisons to both Winston Churc

stories.title: Weather tracker: Cold spells in Greece and Turkey, and storms in Bangladesh
stories.summary: High winds have hit the South Aegean and heavy rain has fallen in Turkey, but Central Europe has felt summer heat Greece and Turkey have found themselves in the grip of a late-season cold spell this weekend. Conditions will persist over the next few days as an area of low pressure situated over Turkey is pulling in colder, moisture-laden air from the north-east via the Black Sea; this meteorological set up has suppressed temperatures well below where they should be for the time of year. Away fro

stories.title: Czech competitor wins European Yo-Yo Championship in Bratislava
stories.summary: Jakub Dolejs of the Czech Republic claimed the top title in the main 1A category at the European Yo-Yo Championship in Slovakia.

stories.title: „Akkor kellett volna szeretni a hazát, amikor az undorító, aljas, hazug, lejárató kampányokat csinálta” – kiakadtak a reklámszakma arcai Balásy interjúján
stories.summary: Erdős Ákos és Marosi Gergely is keményen kritizálta a cégeit az államnak felajánló NER-üzletembert.

stories.title: Úgy mozognak a magyar bérek, mint ebben az évtizedben még soha
stories.summary: A minimálbér-emelés ellenére a magasabb fizetések gyorsabban nőnek, mint az alacsonyabbak. Ebben a bérek átláthatóságáról szóló szabályozás által kiváltott kiigazításoknak is szerepük lehet.

stories.title: Ügynökügy, mezítlábas foglalkoztatás és a bocsánat
stories.summary: Magyar Péter ígéretet tett arra, hogy nyilvánosságra hozza az ügynökaktákat. Ungváry Krisztián történész szerint fontos tisztázni, hogy mi várható ettől, és mi nem. Egy konkrét eseten át bemutatja, miért kell megváltoztatni az „ügynök” jelentését definiáló törvényt, és miért fontos ma is, hogy ismerjük az állampárti kollaborációk történeteit.

stories.title: Áder János se bízott az Orbán magánhadseregének tartott TEK-ben, Magyar Péter végleg szakít velük
stories.summary: A miniszterelnököt 2010 óta a Terrorelhárítási Központ védi, amit Orbán Viktor volt személyi testőre vezet. Magyar Péter már előre jelezte: nem kér belőlük. Orbánnak még egy évig jár a rendőri védelem.

stories.title: A Caesar-mániás maffiózó, akit elárult a jobbkeze, és a munkahelyén szúrták le
stories.summary: Mint sok mai férfit, Salvatore Maranzanót is lenyűgözte a Római Birodalom, de több múltbéli hibából sem tanult, és ez lett a veszte.

stories.title: Magyarországon is érezhető földrengés volt Horvátországban
stories.summary: A 4,2-es magnitúdójú földrengés a magyar határtól 20 kilométerre keletkezett.

stories.title: Karácsony reméli, hogy a buligate-nél komolyabb ügyeket is megoldanak majd Magyar Péterrel
stories.summary: A főpolgármester azt mondta, hogy ő nem akar kormányülésekre járni, de érdemi egyeztetést szeretne az új kormánnyal a város ügyeiről.

stories.title: Nem kell visszafizetnie Orbán Gáspárnak a sandhursti képzés árát
stories.summary: A leköszönő miniszterelnök fia már a választások előtt benyújtotta a leszerelési kérelmét.

stories.title: Segítünk Balásy Gyulának: 92 milliárd forint osztalékot fizettek a cégei 2017 óta
stories.summary: Az államnak felajánlott négy vállalat 2017 előtt nem fizetett érdemi osztalékot, azóta összesen 92,5 milliárd forint nyereséget vettek ki belőlük a tulajdonosok. Nagyrészt maga Balásy Gyula.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Orbán Viktor az utolsó héten még teljesen kifosztaná az államkasszát
stories.summary: Magyar állítása szerint birtokukba jutott egy kormányhatározat, ami alapján a leköszönő Orbán-kormány új kötelezettségeket vállalna, minél nagyobb összegben.

stories.title: Még csak május van, de már több tűz is volt a budai erdőkben
stories.summary: A kis-hárs-hegyi Makovecz Imre-kilátó és a Csergezán Pál-kilátó közelében is oltani kellett.

stories.title: „Elkezdtem könyörögni, hogy halálos beteg vagyok” – szűkös a hozzáférés itthon a forradalmi rákgyógyszerhez
stories.summary: Magyarországon csak külön kérelemre kaphatják meg egyes betegek az egyik legmodernebb rákgyógyszert. A Direkt36 kutatásai szerint ráadásul akár heteket is várni kell az elbírálásra, miközben a késlekedésnek súlyos következményei lehetnek a betegekre nézve.

stories.title: Előkerült a Budapesten eltűnt olasz zenész, egy hazafelé tartó vonaton sikerült őt elérni
stories.summary: Davide Luchellit már az az Interpol is kereste, aztán az olasz nagykövetség jelezte a magyar rendőröknek, hogy a hozzátartozói kapcsolatba tudtak lépni vele.

stories.title: Jókai nem hozott lázba, az irodalmi feladatlap pedig a szívatásra megy – diákok a magyarérettségiről
stories.summary: A magyarérettségiről távozó fiatalokat kérdeztük az idei feladatsorról. A verselemzésnek sokan örültek, az irodalmi műveltségi teszt pedig továbbra is nagy mumus.

stories.title: Magyar Péter bejelentette, kiket jelöl a Tisza az országgyűlés alelnökeinek
stories.summary: Három alelnököt jelölnek a tisztségre.

stories.title: Sulyok visszaszólt Orbánnak, aki nem hajlandó végrehajtani az Európai Bíróság döntését
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök korábban közölte, nem hajtja végre a kormány a gyermekvédelmi törvény ügyében született döntést.

stories.title: Fico: A három testőr a negyedikre vár és a V4-ek újjászületésére
stories.summary: Közös képet tett ki Jerevánból a szlovák, cseh és lengyel miniszterelnök, akik a magyar kormányfőt hiányolták.

stories.title: „Orbán egy betegség volt, amire fel kellett találnunk az ellenszert, és most megvan”
stories.summary: Az oroszok trójai falova volt az EU-ban Orbán az Európai Parlament liberális és néppárti képviselői szerint, akik egyértelműen a jogállam újjáépítésében bíznak a Tisza Párt győzelmével. EP-képviselőket kérdeztünk Strasbourgban.

stories.title: Magyar Péter kivizsgáltatja az NKA pályázatait, ha kell, feljelentést is tesznek
stories.summary: Szerinte nem voltak meggyőzőek Hankó Balázs szavai.

stories.title: A Horn-, a Gyurcsány- és az Orbán-kormányok NKA-vezetői közös nyilatkozatban aggódnak a szervezet jövőjéért
stories.summary: A kultúrafinanszírozás alapelveit rögzítő dokumentum egyik aláírója L. Simon László.

stories.title: Hadházy pofája leszakadt, Radnai Márk felháborodott és Magyar Péter magát idézte a Balásy-interjú után
stories.summary: A cégeit és vagyona egy részét az államra hagyó, könnyek közt beszélő NER-vállalkozót kritizálta több politikus és elemző is.

stories.title: 300 millió forintot kapott Hankó Balázstól Orbán Ráhel ismerősének immerzív színháza
stories.summary: Az egyik legnagyobb támogatási összeg folyt be hozzájuk.

stories.title: Trump claims hostilities in Iran 'have terminated' in letter sent to top US lawmakers
stories.summary: Trump sidestepped a legal deadline for gaining approval from members of Congress over his war in Iran as he sends a letter to top lawmakers claiming that hostilities have ended due to the ongoing ceasefire.

stories.title: Pope appoints former undocumented immigrant as bishop of West Virginia
stories.summary: Leo, who has criticized Trump’s hardline immigration policy, selected Evelio Menjivar-Ayala as state’s new bishop Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Pope Leo XIV has appointed a man who had once entered the United States as an undocumented immigrant, hidden in the trunk of a car, as the new bishop of West Virginia . The pope approved the resignation of Bishop Mark E Brennan of Wheeling-Charleston, West Virginia, and selected Bishop Evelio Menjivar-Ayala

stories.title: Hungary: What will become of Orban and his system?
stories.summary: Outgoing Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban and his Fidesz party are still reeling from their election defeat earlier this month. There is much speculation about what their political future looks like.

stories.title: Keir Starmer braces for his week from hell
stories.summary: LONDON — If you thought Keir Starmer was in trouble now, just wait for Thursday’s elections. The opinion polls would have to be “dramatically out” for the British PM — already hit by dire favorability ratings, a scandal over his pick for U.S. ambassador, and deep disenchantment among his own MPs — to avoid a terrible set of results in nationwide votes held May 7, top psephologist John Curtice, a professor at the University of Strathclyde, says. With local votes taking place across swathes of Eng

stories.title: Merz’s strategy for dealing with Trump’s anger: Tell him he’s right
stories.summary: BERLIN — Facing new tariffs from U.S. President Donald Trump on Germany’s prized auto industry — immediately after his threat to withdraw American troops — Chancellor Friedrich Merz has offered a disarmingly conciliatory response: Trump is essentially right. Since Merz broke from his usual measured script to sharply criticize the Trump administration’s handling of the Iran war last week, an angered U.S. president has retaliated by targeting Germany’s weak spots: its reliance on the U.S. fo

stories.title: Secret Service shoots armed man near White House
stories.summary: Secret Service officers shot a man during an exchange of gunfire Monday near the White House in an incident that wounded a child who happened to be in the area. The incident near the Washington Monument comes amid increased concerns about political violence about a week after a gunman  attempted to breach the security perimeter  at the annual White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington in  an alleged effort  to assassinate President Donald Trump. Monday’s episode unfolde

stories.title: US and Iranian boat attacks threaten ceasefire
stories.summary: The U.S. attacked Iranian boats in the Strait of Hormuz on Monday after Tehran targeted Navy ships, threatening to upend a fragile ceasefire and President Donald Trump’s new vow to open the critical pathway. The U.S. military “blew up” six small Iranian boats after its forces launched “multiple cruise missiles, drones and small boats” at the ships but did not hit them, according to U.S. Central Command head Adm. Bradley Cooper, who leads America’s operations in the region. He did not say what im

stories.title: California seeks millions in penalties from State Farm over alleged claims violations after LA wildfires
stories.summary: State’s top insurance regulator says insurance company violated law while handling claims from 2025 wildfires California is seeking millions of dollars in penalties from State Farm after an investigation found the insurance company was slow to investigate and underpaid claims from the 2025 Los Angeles-area wildfires , regulators announced on Monday. State Farm violated the law hundreds of times in a sampling of 220 cases, the state’s insurance commissioner, Ricardo Lara, said. The maximum penalt

stories.title: US supreme court expedites Voting Rights Act ruling so Louisiana can redraw its maps for midterms
stories.summary: Ketanji Brown Jackson blasted the decision, saying the court has hastened it ruling only twice before in 25 years The US supreme court went out of its way on Monday to help Louisiana Republicans redraw their congressional maps before this year’s midterm elections by allowing a recent ruling that gutted a key part of the Voting Rights Act to take effect ahead of schedule. The procedural move comes less than a week after the court’s landmark decision striking down Louisiana’s congressional map and

stories.title: First Russian oil reportedly arrives in Japan since Iran war – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Trump threatens to blow Iran ‘off the face of the earth’ if it attacks US vessels Trump sends warships to break Iran’s strait of Hormuz blockade We have a bit more of the statement from Maj Gen Ali Abdollahi, the commander of Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya central headquarters, who said earlier that the US or any other foreign armed forces would be attacked if they entered the strait of Hormuz (see post at 07.39 for more details). stories.title: RBA interest rates: Michele Bullock says Australians poorer with ‘no way out’ as she warns of more rate hikes
stories.summary: Reserve Bank of Australia raises cash rate for third consecutive meeting as governor says increases ‘are not going to do anything for inflation’ in next six months Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Use our loan calculator to see how rate hikes could affect you The Reserve Bank governor has said surging oil prices in the wake of the US-Israel war on Iran have made Australians poorer with “no way out”, after handing

stories.title: Jefferson Lewis excused from appearing in court over alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby
stories.summary: Lewis, 47, did not appear at first hearing since being charged over death of girl, 5, who was allegedly abducted from an Alice Springs town camp Warning: This article contains references to Indigenous Australians who have died Jefferson Lewis, the man accused of murdering five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby near a town camp on the outskirts of Alice Springs in the Northern Territory last week, has been excused from his first court appearance. Lewis, 47, was expected to appear in the Alice Spring

stories.title: Reform UK plan to set up migrant detention centres in Green-voting areas condemned by other parties – as it happened
stories.summary: Nigel Farage’s party proposed to place detention centres in places that vote for Green council leaders or MPs Keir Starmer has said that Europe has to face up to the fact that its alliance with the US is under strain. He made the remark in public comments during the plenary session at the European Political Community summit in Yerevan in Armenia. And both of those are impacting all of us in a very material way. In the United Kingdom, if you look at the economic forecast now and compare it to the

stories.title: Starmer lauds £78bn EU loan for Ukraine amid increased Trump tensions
stories.summary: Prime minister uses European Political Community summit to begin negotiations for UK participation in scheme Keir Starmer has said the benefit of joining the European Union’s £78bn loan scheme for Ukraine “outweighs the cost” as he argued the continent must move at pace to bolster its own defence. The prime minister, who said the UK’s involvement in the recovery loan plan would also help create jobs at home, acknowledged that tensions were high between Donald Trump and Europe, particularly over

stories.title: Iran war: UAE reports first Iranian missile and drone attacks in weeks
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates has reported missile and drone attacks from Iran, the first of their kind since the April ceasefire. This comes as the US seeks to help ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Car strikes crowd in central Leipzig
stories.summary: Two people were killed and 20 injured when a car drove into a crowd in central Leipzig. The driver, who police identified as a 33-year-old German national, is in custody as German authorities investigate a motive.

stories.title: Nigeria evacuating 130 citizens from South Africa after anti-migrant protests turn violent
stories.summary: As reports of xenophobic attacks in South Africa continue, Nigeria said it would repatriate its citizens who want to leave.

stories.title: Dubai residents take shelter as UAE intercepts Iranian missile and drone strike
stories.summary: Euronews journalists reported hearing explosions from air defence intercepts above Dubai after a missile alert on Monday, as the UAE said Iran struck an ADNOC-linked tanker in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Can Europe gain an edge on AI and tech — live updates
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — As Europe grapples with the future of artificial intelligence, seeks to rein in platforms and resets its relationship with Washington and Beijing, POLITICO is convening decisionmakers to discuss the policy choices and tradeoffs happening right now. With the ink on the EU’s landmark digital regulations barely dry, what impact will the deregulation agenda have on AI and technology in Europe? How are conversations on the infrastructure needed playing out around Europe? Does Brussels have

stories.title: Norwegian fish farms polluting fjords with waste likened to ‘raw sewage of millions of people’
stories.summary: Exclusive: ‘Fish sludge’ in coastal waters now has nutrient levels equivalent to those in untreated effluent of country the size of Australia, report finds Norwegian fish farms are filling fjords and other coastal waters with nutrient pollution equivalent to the raw sewage of tens of millions of people each year, a report has found . Norway is the largest farmed salmon producer in the world , and nutrients in fish feed are excreted directly into coastal waters. Analysis from the Sunstone Institu

stories.title: Person shot by law enforcement near Washington monument, Secret Service says
stories.summary: The person’s condition and the circumstances surrounding the shooting were not immediately known An individual was shot by law enforcement officers on Monday near the Washington monument, the Secret Service said. The shooting occurred shortly after JD Vance’s motorcade passed through the area. The individual was first identified by plainclothes officers, who said he looked suspicious and was believed to be carrying a firearm, according to Matthew Quinn, the deputy director of the Secret Service.

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Duelling ceasefires as Zelenskyy floats open-ended truce
stories.summary: Vladimir Putin wants empty skies on Friday and Saturday to celebrate Victory Day; Ukrainian president says guns can fall silent by Wednesday if Russia reciprocates. What we know on day 1,532 Continue reading...

stories.title: British crew member in need of urgent medical care amid suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
stories.summary: WHO says seven confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus on MV Hondius, including three passengers who died What is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship? A British crew member was in need of urgent medical care and a passenger from the UK remained in a critical but stable condition following a suspected outbreak of hantavirus on a luxury cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean. Three people have died and medics on Monday were scrambling to evacuate two others from the

stories.title: Democrats say ‘fight is just beginning’ after supreme court’s temporary reinstatement of abortion pill access – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Sign up for the Breaking News US email The supreme court issued a temporary order to restore access to the abortion pill mifepristone by mail on Monday. This comes after two companies who manufacture mifepristone the drug filed an emergency appeal to the court on Saturday asking it to halt a court decision that would require an in‑person exam before the medication can be prescribed. Continue reading...

stories.title: Australian author Craig Silvey pleads guilty to possessing and distributing child exploitation material
stories.summary: Prosecutors drop two other charges against Jasper Jones author, 43, including allegations he produced child exploitation material Award‑winning author Craig Silvey has pleaded guilty to possessing and distributing child exploitation material, while a charge that he produced such material has been dropped. Silvey, 43, was first charged in January after detectives from Western Australia police’s child abuse squad raided his Fremantle home, allegedly catching him communicating online with child exp

stories.title: Concierge firm co-founded by queen’s nephew went on ‘ill-timed’ hiring spree before Iran war
stories.summary: Quintessentially almost quadrupled staff in Middle East and Asia less than year before wealthy began to flee Gulf The embattled luxury concierge service co-founded by Queen Camilla’s nephew Ben Elliot embarked on what appeared to be an inopportune hiring spree in the Middle East and Asia before wealthy individuals began fleeing the region because of the US-Israel war on Iran. Quintessentially almost quadrupled staff in the regions from 22 to 84 during its financial year to 30 April 2025, accordi

stories.title: Australia eyes security pact with Fiji as pushback from Beijing undermines agreement with Vanuatu
stories.summary: Foreign minister Penny Wong visits Fiji this week to progress the security and economic agreement Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia looks close to signing a landmark security and economic agreement with Fiji as part of the Albanese government’s efforts to contain China’s growing influence across the Pacific. But pushback from Beijing has undermined a separate pact with Vanuatu’s government, resulting in a

stories.title: Seen a ghost? The eeriest images from Fotografia Europea – in pictures
stories.summary: From gigantic goat hair costumes to small hidden rooms in houses, this year’s photography festival takes a turn for the spectral Continue reading...

stories.title: Salisbury Cathedral restores stained-glass treasure by Edward Burne-Jones and William Morris
stories.summary: Project to refurbish ‘Angels’ hailed huge success as dean says it will ‘lift spirits and rekindle hope’ Sam Kelly admitted there had been some nights of broken sleep as he led a two-year project to restore one of Salisbury Cathedral’s treasures, a stained-glass window by Edward Burne-Jones and William Morris. “It would be wrong to say I didn’t lie awake sometimes,” said the head glazier. “You are working on something that is very precious and if it fell on the floor, it would be very bad.” Conti

stories.title: Anger over green light for traffic lights on Argyll’s ‘Bridge over the Atlantic’
stories.summary: Plans to install traffic lights on Clachan Bridge will ruin tourist attraction and endanger pedestrians, critics warn Traffic lights are to be installed beside the scenic Clachan Bridge on Scotland’s wild Argyll coast despite fears it will be a “desecration” of a nationally-significant heritage site. Known since the early 19th century as the “Bridge over the Atlantic” the 233-year-old crossing from the mainland to the Isle of Seil attracts visitors eager to boast of their trans-oceanic journey,

stories.title: HSBC profits fall amid $400m fraud-related charge and Iran war
stories.summary: London-headquartered bank’s shares slide as it sets aside an extra $300m to cover effects of Middle East conflict Business live – latest updates HSBC has taken a $1.3bn (£961m) hit to profits, fuelled by the fallout from the US-Israel war on Iran and fraud in the troubled private credit sector . The London-headquartered bank said profits fell 4% in the first three months of the year, dropping $100m to $9.4bn, compared with the same period in 2025. Revenue increased 6% to $18.6bn. Continue readin

stories.title: Vodafone to take full control of UK mobile operator in £4.3bn deal
stories.summary: Hong Kong conglomerate CK Hutchison agrees to sell 49% stake as it attempts to reshape its portfolio Business live – latest updates Vodafone is to take full control of the UK’s biggest mobile operator in a £4.3bn buyout deal with the Hong Kong conglomerate CK Hutchison. The billionaire Li Ka-shing’s business said it had agreed to sell its 49% stake in VodafoneThree – a network with more than 27 million subscribers – to its partner Vodafone. Continue reading...

stories.title: Starmer calls antisemitism ‘crisis for all of us’ ahead of Downing Street summit – UK politics live
stories.summary: The prime minister said the attack in Golders Green is ‘part of a pattern of rising antisemitism’ in the country Good morning. Keir Starmer has declared antisemitism “a crisis for all of us”. This morning he is hosting an event in Downing Street intended to get leaders across all areas of public life to address the problem and, according to the briefing from No 10, he will say: Last week’s terrorist attack in Golders Green was utterly appalling. But it was not an isolated incident. It is part of

stories.title: Michele Bullock says ‘we are poorer and there is no way out of it’ after rate rise – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Two marine rescue volunteers who went to help others are among those who have died in a tragic yacht accident on Monday night. As we reported below, three people died as volunteers went to the aid of a yacht at the Ballina bar in northern New South Wales. Continue reading...

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: ‘We have not even begun’, Iran warns US amid escalation in strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Iranian speaker says ‘new equation of the strait of Hormuz is in the process of being solidified’ Donald Trump sends warships to break Iran’s strait of Hormuz blockade The internet blackout in Iran has entered its 67th day, according to internet monitoring group NetBlocks, as the regime continues one of the longest-running national internet shutdowns ever recorded. NetBlocks said in a social media post : The internet blackout in Iran is now entering its 67th day after passing incident hour 1584.

stories.title: Germany’s military power is on the rise. This time it must be firmly embedded in Europe | Timothy Garton Ash
stories.summary: As Russian aggression continues and Trump’s US threatens Nato, it is even more vital for a unified defence of the continent As we mark the 81st anniversary of the end of the second world war in Europe this Friday, 8 May, it’s clear that Germany will again be the leading European military power. Already next year its defence spending will be as much as that of France and Britain combined – and it is projected to be significantly larger by 2030. The German government’s declared goal is to have the

stories.title: 'Deeply shocked': Leipzig mayor reacts after car rams crowd in Germany – video
stories.summary: At least two people have been killed and several injured after a driver in an SUV ploughed into a crowd in the centre of Leipzig in eastern Germany, the city’s mayor has said. 'The police have apprehended the suspected assailant,' Burkhard Jung said on Monday, adding that the authorities had the scene in a pedestrian zone under control. 'We still don’t really know the motivation. We don’t know anything about the perpetrator.' Emergency services workers said about two dozen people were hurt in th

stories.title: Person shot near Washington Monument, US Secret Service says
stories.summary: The White House was briefly locked down on Monday after the US Secret Service said an individual was shot by law enforcement near the Washington Monument.

stories.title: Leipzig: 2 killed after car drives into crowd
stories.summary: Around 20 other people were injured, including three seriously. The driver, a 33-year-old German national, was detained at the scene in the center of the eastern German city.

stories.title: China: Explosion at fireworks factory kills at least 21
stories.summary: A blast at a fireworks factory in China has killed 21 people and wounded 61 others. President Xi Jinping has called for a thorough probe.

stories.title: Iran war: Germany condemns attacks on UAE
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has urged Iran to return to the negotiating table after the UAE was targeted by drone and missile attacks. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Taiwan's Lai returns from Eswatini trip amid China objection
stories.summary: Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te has arrived home from the tiny African kingdom of Eswatini after three China-friendly Indian Ocean countries denied him permission to fly through their airspace.

stories.title: India news: West Bengal wakes to a new political reality
stories.summary: After years of making steady inroads, Narendra Modi's Bharatiya Janata Party has clinched West Bengal at the polls. Meanwhile, India's foreign ministry condemned Iran over an attack on the UAE. Follow live.

stories.title: Russia offers Ukraine May 8-9 ceasefire amid WWII anniversary, but threatens major reprisals in same breath
stories.summary: President Putin has unilaterally declared a two-day truce amid commemorations of the end of World War II in Moscow. Ukraine's President Zelenskyy proposed his own truce starting a couple of days earlier.

stories.title: Robots move in as waste firms struggle to find staff
stories.summary: Humanoid robots are being added to the automation of waste sorting.

stories.title: Second hantavirus case confirmed after three die in suspected cruise ship outbreak
stories.summary: The operator of the MV Hondius ship says a Dutch couple and a German national have died.

stories.title: Watch: Met Gala sees Beyoncé's return and a star-studded red carpet
stories.summary: Anna Wintour brought the glam, Beyoncé brought the fam, and Heidi Klum was... statuesque at this year's fundraiser.

stories.title: Rescuers among three dead after yacht sinks off Australian coast
stories.summary: Volunteer rescuers aged 78 and 62 died after their boat rolled during "treacherous" conditions.

stories.title: Two people killed and two in critical condition in suspected deliberate vehicle ramming in Leipzig
stories.summary: Two people have been confirmed dead while two others remain hospitalised and in critical condition after a man rammed a vehicle into a crowd of people in a busy shopping district in the eastern city of Leipzig.

stories.title: Deadly China plane crash was caused by fuel cut-off, says report
stories.summary: The Boeing 737 plane crashed into a Chinese hillside in 2022, killing all 132 people on board.

stories.title: Two killed and many injured after car driven into crowd in German city of Leipzig
stories.summary: A 33-year-old German citizen was detained following the incident, the authorities say.

stories.title: 21 people killed and dozens injured in massive explosion at fireworks plant in China
stories.summary: Authorities are still investigating the reasons of the explosion, with negligence being suspected as a likely cause. Officials warned that the death toll is likely to climb as search and rescue operations continue.

stories.title: Europe is hungry for AI data centres — but its energy grid cannot feed them
stories.summary: From decade-long grid queues to facilities running at half capacity, a new study exposes the energy crisis at the heart of Europe's push to boost its AI capabilities.

stories.title: Popular Australian author pleads guilty over child exploitation material
stories.summary: The Jasper Jones and Runt author was arrested in January during a police raid at his Perth home.

stories.title: Explosion at China fireworks factory kills 26 people
stories.summary: A blast at a fireworks factory in Hunan province has killed 26 people and injured 61, state media report.

stories.title: Macron sings Aznavour song as Armenian PM Pashinyan plays drums at official dinner
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron performed Charles Aznavour’s “La Bohème” during an official dinner in Armenia, accompanied by Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan on drums.

stories.title: Mourners lay flowers and candles in Leipzig after deadly car rampage
stories.summary: People gather in Leipzig to lay flowers and light candles near the site where a car ploughed into a crowd in the historic centre, killing two people and injuring several others.

stories.title: A 'fun' superstar stuns rivals and reshapes politics in an Indian state
stories.summary: Film star Vijay has defied naysayers to almost sweep Tamil Nadu polls, marking a break in the political order.

stories.title: Tehran regime executes three protesters, one 'without evidence'
stories.summary: Iran’s judiciary says three protesters detained over the January unrest in the city of Mashhad have been hanged, as rights groups report four murder convicts were executed in a unrelated case in Isfahan.

stories.title: Watch: Moment United Airlines flight strikes vehicle during landing
stories.summary: Flight 169 from Venice, Italy, landed safely at Newark Airport with no reported injuries among the 231 passengers and crew. The lorry driver sustained minor injuries.

stories.title: Celebrations as Modi’s ruling party poised to take control of India's West Bengal state
stories.summary: Supporters of Indian prime minister Narendra Modi's Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) celebrate as trends released by the Election Commission show BJP leading in 176 out of 294 seats in West Bengal.

stories.title: Armenia tests Moscow’s patience with Zelenskyy visit and EU leaders’ summit
stories.summary: One month before the highly anticipated parliamentary elections, Armenia’s Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan set out the country’s foreign policy trajectory with striking clarity, hosting European leaders, the NATO secretary general, and even Ukraine’s president.

stories.title: Mali junta leader names himself defence minister after predecessor killed
stories.summary: Former Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in a massive offensive by combined jihadist and separatist forces.

stories.title: Ukrainian drone hits upmarket Moscow high-rise ahead of Victory Day celebrations
stories.summary: Russia has declared a unilateral ceasefire to cover the event and threatened a massive strike on central Kyiv if it were disrupted.

stories.title: Euronews journalist reports Emirates flight to Dubai held at Heathrow during UAE air strikes
stories.summary: Toby Gregory reported the atmosphere in the cabin remained calm, with passengers having no concerns about safety due to Emirates' track record of maintaining passenger safety.

stories.title: Japan sees largest protest in support of pacifist constitution as PM Takaichi pushes revisions
stories.summary: Japanese leader Sanae Takaichi has called for discussions to revise the constitution, saying it should ‘reflect the demands of the times’ Japan’s prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, has called for “advanced discussions” on revising the pacifist constitution, as large demonstrations were held nationwide to oppose any changes to the country’s supreme law. Speaking during an official visit to Vietnam, Takaichi said the constitution, which was written by US occupation forces after the second world war,

stories.title: Austria expels three Russian embassy staff after ‘forest of antennae’ discovered
stories.summary: Austrian foreign minister says Russian diplomatic mission in Vienna was being used for illicit collection of data Austria has expelled three Russian embassy staff on suspicion of spying after determining that a “forest of antennae” on the diplomatic mission in Vienna, Europe’s espionage capital since the cold war, was being be used for illicit data collection. “It is unacceptable that diplomatic immunity be used to commit espionage,” Austria’s foreign minister, Beate Meinl-Reisinger, said on Mon

stories.title: ‘Project Freedom’: a grand humanitarian gesture, or a fast track to more war?
stories.summary: After banging his battle drum, Trump is back in Nobel peace prize mode. But the move has dramatically raised the stakes “Project Freedom” has all the trappings of a classic episode of the Trump Show, the reality series that the rest of the world does not just have to watch, but live through and survive. It has a dramatic plot twist, it is bathed in a self-projected beatific light, and the trailer looked far more promising that the reality. Trump spent a long weekend in Florida banging the war dr

stories.title: Narendra Modi’s BJP wins election in West Bengal for the first time
stories.summary: Result in key Indian state is set to have significant implications for the country’s political landscape Narendra Modi’s party has won a resounding election victory in West Bengal, a state which had been a rare opposition stronghold, expanding his unrivalled consolidation of power across the country. It is the first time that the Indian prime minister’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) has won assembly elections in West Bengal, a large and politically significant state in eastern India. Continue rea

stories.title: Two killed and several hurt after car ploughs into crowds in German city of Leipzig
stories.summary: A suspect has been apprehended, but detectives say little is known about their motivation at this stage At least two people have been killed and several injured after a driver in an SUV ploughed into a crowd in the centre of Leipzig in eastern Germany, the city’s mayor has said. “The police have apprehended the suspected assailant,” Burkhard Jung said on Monday, adding that the authorities had the scene in a pedestrian zone under control. “We still don’t really know the motivation. We don’t know

stories.title: Donald Trump sends warships to break Iran’s strait of Hormuz blockade
stories.summary: US operation announced as ‘Project Freedom’ dramatically raises stakes in conflict The US has launched Donald Trump’s operation to open a route through the strait of Hormuz for hundreds of ships trapped with their crews in the Gulf, in a move that brought the region back to the brink of full-scale war as Iran sought to reassert its blockade. The US operation, which got under way on Monday after being announced as “Project Freedom” by Trump on Sunday night on his social media site, dramatically r

stories.title: Women's Champions League: Bayern fall to Barcelona in semis
stories.summary: Bayern Munich have come up short in their bid to win the Women's Champions League for the first time after losing to Barcelona in the semifinals.

stories.title: Angine de Poitrine: A masked rock band conquers the internet
stories.summary: From underground experiment to sold-out international concerts within days — as the French-Canadian band Angine de Poitrine starts touring the world, we take a look at the viral phenomenon.

stories.title: EPC summit: Europe has 'gotten the message' from Trump
stories.summary: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said at the opening of the EPC summit in Armenia that Europe has "heard" Washington's frustration over its conflict with Iran and are "stepping up."

stories.title: Georg Baselitz, artist renowned for his upside-down paintings, dies
stories.summary: He was thrown out of art school at the age of 18 because of his love for Picasso. The German artist who never lost his rebellious streak has died at the age of 88.

stories.title: Decoding China: Beijing courts Europe amid US dysfunction
stories.summary: China is hoping to secure new allies for a future multipolar world order as the partnership between the US and the European powers slides into a crisis.

stories.title: India news: Modi claims victory in West Bengal election
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's Hindu nationalist BJP party is poised to govern the state for the first time ever. Modi hopes to make inroads into other opposition strongholds too.

stories.title: How child soldiers in Sudan become influencers on TikTok
stories.summary: Some Sudanese child soldiers have social media accounts with millions of followers. Psychologists and other experts warn that this is a dangerous trend.

stories.title: Pushback in Nigeria over ex-Boko Haram fighter reintegration
stories.summary: Nigeria is set to reintegrate more than 700 former Boko Haram fighters into society under a government deradicalization program. Authorities say this is key to ending the insurgency — but communities are skeptical.

stories.title: Dubai's image as a financial hub faces its biggest test yet
stories.summary: Amid the smoldering Iran war, wealthy elites are quietly shifting capital and other assets out of Dubai and heading to Singapore and Switzerland. Just how resilient is the emirate's safe-haven reputation?

stories.title: Participants compete to lure worms from the ground at quirky UK festival
stories.summary: Contestants gathered in southern England for the annual “Wormcharming” competition, attempting to coax worms from the ground without digging.

stories.title: Humanoid robots deployed to direct traffic in China
stories.summary: Humanoid robots have been deployed at intersections in the Chinese city of Hangzhou to assist traffic police and guide pedestrians.

stories.title: Trump says Iran has 'taken some shots' but caused little damage in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Meanwhile, a senior admiral said the US had destroyed six Iranian boats and shot down missiles and drones fired at US Navy and commercial vessels by Tehran's forces.

stories.title: Modi's BJP conquers Bengal, one of India's toughest political frontiers
stories.summary: West Bengal’s verdict could reshape not just the state’s politics, but the future trajectory of Modi’s BJP.

stories.title: Competitors race and jump at Germany’s Hobby Horse Championship
stories.summary: Participants rode stick horses through jumping and dressage events during Germany’s second national Hobby Horse Championship in Frankfurt.

stories.title: Shipping giant MSC opens new trade route to bypass Hormuz disruption
stories.summary: As tensions choke the Strait of Hormuz, Mediterranean Shipping Company races to keep global trade moving with new route.

stories.title: German minehunter heads to Mediterranean for possible Hormuz mission
stories.summary: Germany’s minehunter Fulda departed from Kiel for the Mediterranean as Berlin considers a possible future deployment in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Former New York City mayor Rudy Giuliani recovering from pneumonia and 'now breathing on his own'
stories.summary: The ex-New York mayor 'remains in critical but stable condition', according to his spokesman.

stories.title: Three Russian diplomats expelled from Austria over spying accusations
stories.summary: They gathered information using a "forest of antennas" installed on top of diplomatic buildings, the foreign minister says.

stories.title: Diplomats survey Karabakh reconstruction as peace settles in
stories.summary: European and US diplomats surveyed Azerbaijan’s reconstruction of Karabakh and expressed support for the peace between Armenia and Azerbaijan settling on the region gripped by decades of conflict until 2025.

stories.title: Britney Spears pleads guilty to reckless driving after arrest
stories.summary: The 44-year-old will avoid jail time after her arrest in California earlier this year.

stories.title: Spain seizes record amount of cocaine in Atlantic Ocean, authorities say
stories.summary: The Civil Guard found between 30,000 and 45,000kg of the drug on a freighter headed to Libya.

stories.title: Keddi-szerdai tűzszünetet jelentett be Zelenszkij
stories.summary: Az oroszok a Győzelem Napja környékén, pénteken és szombaton hirdettek fegyvernyugvást.

stories.title: Az utolsó utáni pillanatban mentett pontot a Manchester City, de így is elúszhatott a bajnoki cím
stories.summary: Őrült meccsen játszottak 3-3-at az Evertonnal, így ha az Arsenal megnyeri a maradék három meccsét, biztosan bajnok.

stories.title: Örül, hogy vége a kampánynak? Sok településen hamarosan újra választások jönnek
stories.summary: Az összeférhetetlenségi szabályok miatt Hajdúböszörményben, Kadarkúton, Sárváron, Békéscsabán, Mohácson, Mezőtúron és Makón kell majd időközi választást kiírni.

stories.title: Évek óta jelezték problémáikat a Bárczi távozó oktatói, akik most egy másik képzésben folytatják a munkát
stories.summary: Az ELTE gyógypedagógiai karának oktatói úgy érezték, nem volt mozgásterük.

stories.title: Nem lesz per, Blake Lively és Justin Baldoni megállapodtak a zaklatási ügyben
stories.summary: Diplomatikus közös közleményt adtak ki, amiben jelezték, sikerült felhívni a figyelmet a zaklatások áldozataira.

stories.title: Robbanás történt egy kínai tűzijátékgyárban, 21 ember meghalt
stories.summary: A robbanás a tűzijátékgyártás egyik központjában történt.

stories.title: Dunakeszi vasútgyártás: a mentési próbálkozásba még tízmilliárdokat kéne beleölni, de ha nem tesszük, még nagyobb veszteség lehet a vége
stories.summary: Az állam szeretné megmenteni a hazai vasútgyártást, és leszállítana Egyiptomnak 350 vasúti kocsit. Csak éppen fél éve nem sikerül újraindítani az üzemet, a kormányváltás miatt pedig új embereknek kellene átlátniuk a helyzetet, miközben szorít az idő.

stories.title: Tarr Zoltán a Balásy-interjúra: Most, hogy a süllyedő hajón megcsúszott a lábuk, hirtelen felfedezték a bűnbánatot
stories.summary: Márki-Zay Péter azt írta, hogy a „Mini Ferit”, azaz őt nem hatották meg Balásy Gyula könnycseppjei.

stories.title: A Fidesz-közeli Századvégnél is leépítés kezdődik
stories.summary: Azt írták, a jövőben is „független elemző intézményként” folytatják, de már kisebb létszámmal.

stories.title: „Eltűrjük mi ezt?” – kérdezi a Pesti Srácok főszerkesztője, aki szerint Balásyt kényszerítették
stories.summary: Huth Gergely szerint bárki lehet a következő, ami pedig most zajlik, az „egy jó előre eltervezett államcsíny”.

stories.title: Szeppentél
Matekérettségi: másodfokú egyenlet és függvény elemzés a feladatlap második részében
stories.summary: A diákok reggel 9-től délig írhatják a középszintű matematikaérettségit.

stories.title: Megkongatták a vészharangot az irodalmi lapok, a megszűnés fenyegeti őket
stories.summary: Tíz folyóirat nyílt levélben sürgeti az NKA által megítélt támogatások lehető leggyorsabb folyósítását.

stories.title: Two US service members missing after military exercises in Morocco
stories.summary: Search and rescue operation launched after service members reported missing near south-western city of Tan Tan Two US service members are missing in south-western Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (Africom) said on Sunday. The US, Morocco and other countries participating in the African Lion exercise have launched a search and rescue operation, Africom said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canada to be first non-European nation at EPC summit as Carney seeks allies
stories.summary: Attendance at European Political Community meeting in Yerevan seen as part of effort to build ties after US rupture Canada is to become the first non-European country to attend a meeting of the European Political Community when the prime minister, Mark Carney, joins Monday’s summit of the 48-plus nation grouping in Yerevan, Armenia. Carney has said he is determined to build a new network of trade and diplomatic alliances after the loss of US markets under Donald Trump. His presence will also rep

stories.title: Three passengers dead after suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
stories.summary: Dutch husband and wife and third unidentified person reported to have died, with three further people taken ill What is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship? A suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus infection on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean killed three people – including an elderly married couple – and sickened at least three others, the World Health Organisation (WHO) and South Africa’s health department said on Sunday. The WHO said an investigation w

stories.title: The centre left is not dead. A progressive new counter-Trumpian movement is on the way | Florian Ranft
stories.summary: Social democrats are at last facing up to the failures of globalisation to create equality or deliver for workers If Donald Trump represents the backlash against the liberal rules-based order, then we may now be seeing the backlash to the backlash. In a recent speech , the Spanish prime minister, Pedro Sánchez, spoke of just that. “They scream and shout not because they are winning, but because they know their time is running out,” he said , of those seeking to undermine international law and no

stories.title: Flaws in Kenya’s AI-driven health reforms driving up costs for the poorest
stories.summary: Exclusive : amid unrest, President William Ruto promised to give all Kenyans access to healthcare. But the algorithm favours the rich, an investigation has found An AI system used to predict how much Kenyans can afford to pay for access to healthcare, has systemically driven up costs for the poor, an investigation has found. The healthcare system being rolled out across the country, a key electoral promise of President William Ruto, was launched in October 2024 and intended to replace Kenya’s de

stories.title: How Serbia's government dominates the country's media
stories.summary: Since Serbia's ruling SNS party came to power in 2012, it has tightened its grip on the media. With an election expected soon, experts fear authorities will try to eliminate the last pockets of independent reporting.

stories.title: Trump says Iran has 'not yet paid a big enough price' as he reviews new peace proposal
stories.summary: The US president had earlier in the week refused another Iranian proposal, saying Tehran is not willing to give Washington what it “needs to have” to strike a deal.

stories.title: Iran executes detainee of the 2022 'Woman, Life, Freedom' movement
stories.summary: Iran's judiciary announced on Sunday that it had carried out the death execution of Mehrab Abdollahzadeh, a man arrested during 2022 protest movement, launched after the death of 22-year-old Mahsa Amini while in Iran's morality police custody.

stories.title: Seven killed in southern Lebanon amid Israeli airstrikes and ceasefire tensions
stories.summary: State media reported that several villages were hit as neighbourhoods near the border are being levelled. The IDF maintains that 50 airstrikes conducted over 24 hours specifically targeted Hezbollah infrastructure.

stories.title: Rare comet to flash through New Zealand skies – before it disappears for 170,000 years
stories.summary: Over the next two weeks stargazers have a chance to spot the blue-green orb and smudgy tail of comet known as C/2025 R3 PanSTARRS A comet formed on the edges of the solar system will grace southern skies over the next fortnight, giving viewers a rare chance to glimpse it before it disappears from view for another 170,000 years. The comet – known as C/2025 R3 PanSTARRS – had been travelling through the northern hemisphere but has “swung around the sun” and is now visible in the south, said Josh A

stories.title: Több fideszes kulcsszereplő nem ment el a mandátumátadójára, a Tisza vitázásra képes frakciót ígér
stories.summary: Átvették mandátumukat azok a képviselők, akik országos listáról jutottak be a parlamentbe. Mindhárom párt konstruktív együttműködésben bízik. Az igazságügyiminiszter-jelöltet ért kritikákról a Tisza frakcióvezetője azt mondta: kritikához mindenkinek joga van. A Fidesz volt kampányigazgatója pedig nem érzi úgy, hogy hazudtak volna a kampányban.

stories.title: Feljelentést tett az MNB az Erste Bank megzsarolásával kapcsolatban
stories.summary: Simor András korábbi jegybankelnök állításai után hivatali visszaélés miatt tettek feljelentést.

stories.title: „Idén nem kell szüretelni” – tízmilliárdos károkat okozhat a május elsejei fagy az alföldi szőlészeteken
stories.summary: Évtizedek óta nem látott hideg csapott le, a legsúlyosabb becslések szerint az alföldi szőlő 80 százaléka teljesen megsemmisülhetett.

stories.title: Levágott fülek és félbemaradt szerelem egy nem létező városban
stories.summary: A kínai Jü Hua regénye jóval több, mint történelmi tabló: gondosan kidolgozott szereplői, emberi sorsai és egy tragikus szerelmi történet miatt eleven és olvasmányos elbeszéléssé válik.

stories.title: Hiába tiltotta meg a kormány az ukrajnai gázszállítást
stories.summary: A választási kampány csúcsán kormányhatározat született arról, hogy nem lehet aukciót kiírni az ukrajnai gázszállítás lehetőségére. Ma mégis lezajlott az árverés.

stories.title: A jelenlegi elnök és az Országos Bíró Tanács volt vezetője pályáztak a Fővárosi Törvényszék elnöki posztjára
stories.summary: A Hvg.hu értesülését az Országos Bírósági Hivatal megerősítette a Telexnek, a bírák egy hét múlva szavaznak majd, hogy kit látnának szívesebben a szervezet élén.

stories.title: Tömegbe hajtott egy autó Lipcsében, ketten meghaltak
stories.summary: A sétálóutcán végighaladó autóst elfogták, az esetben sokan megsérültek.

stories.title: Balásy Gyula a cégeit és a magántőkealapokban lévő vagyonát is átadja az államnak
stories.summary: A NER kedvenc médiavállalkozója egy, a Kontrollnak adott interjúban jelentette be ezt.

stories.title: Feketére maszkírozott fehér színész miatt vetette le a Radnai Márk által rendezett darabot egy külföldi ügynökség a Thália Színház műsoráról
stories.summary: Kálomista Gábor színházigazgató jogi úton szerezne érvényt az igazának.

stories.title: Kiderült, miért nem mentek át hétfőn a nagyobb összegű banki utalások
stories.summary: Az ezeket kiszolgáló rendszer, az InterGIRO2 átmeneti lassulása okozta a problémát, derült ki az ezt szolgáltató GIRO Zrt. közleményéből.

stories.title: Wáberer György visszakapta a Tisza Pártnak utalt 100 millió forintját, és továbbutalta Iványi Gáborék közösségének
stories.summary: Wáberer György azt írta a Telexnek, hogy azért öt nappal a választás előtt utalt a Tisza Pártnak, mert akkor személyesen a nyilvánosság előtt is felvállalta, hogy Tisza programját támogatja.

stories.title: Balásy Gyula sírva mondta el, hogy nem szeretné elhagyni az országot, és átadja a vagyonát az államnak
stories.summary: Cégeinek számláit az elmúlt hetekben már befagyasztották, mesélte a Kontrollnak a NER kedvenc médiavállalkozója, a kormány kampányainak kivitelezője. Nem tudja, mi alól kellene tisztáznia magát, minden „agyon volt dokumentálva”, és szerinte nagyon komoly értékeket teremtettek a cégében, ezt szeretné megmenteni azzal, hogy átadja.

stories.title: Kedden matematikával folytatódik az érettségi, vajon mire emlékszik a középiskolai tananyagból?
stories.summary: A vizsga után érdemes lesz a Telex élő adását követni, mert kedden délután Koren Balázs matematikatanár bemutatja néhány érdekesebb feladat megoldását.

stories.title: Pénzbírságot kapott a kecskeméti kollégiumi igazgató, aki három éve egy mém miatt rakott ki egy diákot
stories.summary: Közfeladati helyzettel visszaélés miatt emeltek vádat ellene, az első fokú ítélet szerint 234 ezer forintot kell fizetnie.

stories.title: Novák Előd a fakivágós ügyről: Újbuda önkormányzata évek óta próbálja ellehetetleníteni a majálisunkat
stories.summary: A Mi Hazánk majálisán esett károk miatt felháborodott a XI. kerületi önkormányzat, erre reagált a párt.

stories.title: Germany news: Majority thinks coalition government doomed — survey
stories.summary: More than half of people in Germany believe the coalition government will collapse before its term is up. The head of a leading economic think tank says US tariffs could trigger a German recession.

stories.title: Voters will judge Trump on the economy - how is it doing?
stories.summary: How much Donald Trump can do with the time he has left as president will depend massively on the cost of living.

stories.title: Massive crowds fill Rio's Copacabana beach for free Shakira concert
stories.summary: Latin pop queen Shakira kicks off her free mega-concert on Rio's famed Copacabana beach under a full moon, with drones forming the image of a she-wolf -- her nickname -- overhead as she takes the stage over an hour late.

stories.title: Massive sea lion “Chonkers” draws crowds in San Francisco
stories.summary: A massive Steller sea lion nicknamed “Chonkers” has become a popular attraction at San Francisco’s Fisherman’s Wharf, drawing visitors and social media attention.

stories.title: BBC uncovers the scammers exploiting dogs to elicit donations from animal lovers
stories.summary: Unwitting donors hand over money to save suffering animals but Ugandan con artists pocket the cash.

stories.title: Oil prices edge up despite Trump vowing action in Hormuz tensions
stories.summary: Oil prices rose slightly in early trading on Monday as investors monitored Iran war developments. Brent crude and WTI front month contracts are trading around 1% higher at around $109 and $103 per barrel respectively.

stories.title: Trump follows through on threats as he announces significant troop withdrawal from Germany
stories.summary: Trump had earlier threatened to pull some US troops from Germany after Chancellor Friedrich Merz expressed his disapproval with the US-Israeli war on Iran, which has upended the Middle East and caused a global oil price crisis.

stories.title: Trump jokes that the US Navy will takeover Cuba 'almost immediately' on the way back from Iran
stories.summary: Trump appeared to hint at a potential military operation in Cuba after US forces “finish the job” in Iran. The comments come as Washington announced a set of new sanctions targeting the island nation on Friday, which Havana slammed as a form of collective punishment.

stories.title: Businessman dies after alleged beating in custody over brother’s Starlink detention
stories.summary: Reports from Iran suggest that a citizen named Hesam Alaeeddin, son of the founder of Alaeddin Mall in Tehran, was killed while being taken into custody by security forces.

stories.title: How we reported in -30C temperatures in Canada's Arctic
stories.summary: BBC's Nadine Yousif and Eloise Alanna spent five days travelling with the country's military rangers through frostbite and sub-zero temperatures.

stories.title: German museum to return rare Irritator dinosaur skull to Brazil
stories.summary: Spinosaurid fossil bought by Stuttgart institution in 1991 has been the subject of a long restitution campaign It is a 113-million-year-old bone of contention. After Stuttgart’s museum of natural history bought a fossilised dinosaur skull in 1991, researchers found it was the most complete spinosaurid skull known to date, belonging to a previously unknown genus of the huge meat-eating dinosaurs. Continue reading...

stories.title: Reaching for the stars: enduring symbols of Soviet science – in pictures
stories.summary: The photographer Eric Lusito takes us on a scientific journey through space and time in a book on Soviet scientific institutes Continue reading...

stories.title: Alex Zanardi, F1 driver and multiple CART and Paralympic champion, dies at 59
stories.summary: Italian race driver turned Paralympian Alex Zanardi has died aged 59. The former Formula 1 driver and two-time CART champion lost his legs in a horrific crash at the peak of his powers, but his drive to compete endured.

stories.title: Why Ukraine and Israel are arguing over grain
stories.summary: Cargo ships have docked in Haifa, Israel, carrying grain thought to originate from Russian-occupied parts of Ukraine. Kyiv considers the cargo to be stolen goods — but Israel says evidence is lacking.

stories.title: Austria: Police arrest suspect over poisoned HiPP baby food
stories.summary: A 39-year-old man has been arrested in Austria for his alleged involvement in an attempt to blackmail a German baby food manufacturer.

stories.title: Why Russia's Northern Sea Route is a risk for global trade
stories.summary: Russia wants its Northern Sea Route to become an important artery of global trade. However, the Arctic shortcut between Europe and Asia is fraught with both political and environmental hurdles.

stories.title: Where Germany's economy still excels
stories.summary: German industry is being lapped in key sectors like solar panels, semiconductors and even cars — but the country's midsize businesses suggest the economy isn't losing its edge.

stories.title: Millions sway as free Shakira concert lights up Copacabana
stories.summary: The Colombian superstar delivers one of the biggest shows of her career, as fans flock from Brazil and beyond for her "Women No Longer Cry" world tour.

stories.title: Spirit Airlines says it is going out of business and ending operations 'immediately' after 34 years
stories.summary: The budget airline had hoped for a government bailout to arrive on Friday to prevent it from going out of business. The Trump administration announced on Friday that it had offered the airline its final proposal, but a deal could not be reached.

stories.title: Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi hospitalised amid deteriorating health condition
stories.summary: Sources close to the human rights activist say they suspect she had suffered a heart attack in prison and fear her transfer to hospital may be too late.

stories.title: Iran executes two more over 'spying for Israel'
stories.summary: As Iran’s new wave of executions continues, the judiciary chief, defying international calls to halt them, vows no leniency and dismisses 'arrogant' foreign critics.

stories.title: Dragonflies in distress: Scientists sound alarm in India's ecological hotspot
stories.summary: A new study reveals a decline in dragonfly and damselfly species in India's Western Ghats.

stories.title: Kenya battles to stop the 'goons and guns' as fears of political violence grow
stories.summary: More than 100 gangs are operating nationwide as the growing use of political thugs raises fears of election violence.

stories.title: Trump says US will reduce troop presence in Germany 'a lot further' than initial 5,000 withdrawal
stories.summary: Trump indicated without sharing details that a substantial further reduction of US troop presence in Germany was imminent, just a day after the Pentagon announced a withdrawal of 5,000 soldiers. Trump has also threatened earlier in the week to rollback troops in Spain and Italy.

stories.title: Huge crowd attends free Shakira Copacabana beach concert
stories.summary: Shakira's appearance followed shows by Lady Gaga and Madonna, who have also performed on Copacabana beach in previous years.

stories.title: Cuba says Trump’s fresh sanctions on its economy amount to ‘collective punishment’
stories.summary: The US sanctions target people operating in broad sections of Cuban economy, including energy, defence and mining Cuba’s government has said new sanctions imposed on the island by Donald Trump amounted to “collective punishment”, as an enormous 1 May procession outside the American embassy in Havana vowed to “defend the homeland”. In an executive order on Friday, the US president said he would impose sanctions on people involved in broad sections of the Cuban economy, as he seeks to put more pre

stories.title: The significance of US military bases in Germany
stories.summary: President Donald Trump has said he is planning to cut US troop numbers in Germany. How many US bases are there in the country now, and what's their strategic importance?

stories.title: Sri Lanka police arrest 22 Buddhist monks after 110kg of cannabis found in luggage
stories.summary: Customs officials say group allegedly hid 5kg of ‘kush’ in false walls of bags on return from Bangkok holiday Twenty-two Buddhist monks are in Sri Lankan police custody after customs officials found 110kg of high-grade cannabis concealed in their luggage, the largest ever drug bust at Colombo’s main international airport. The group, mostly junior monks in training from temples across Sri Lanka, were alleged to have “carried about five kilos of the narcotic concealed within false walls in their l

stories.title: A new climate club, minus the fossil fuel lobby
stories.summary: Frustrated by blocking coalitions, 50-plus nations at the inaugural talks on exiting fossil fuels made one thing clear: it's all about how — not whether — to phase out coal, oil and gas.

stories.title: Germany news: Greens and CDU agree Baden-Württemberg deal
stories.summary: The state of Baden-Württemberg's Greens and conservatives have forged a coalition deal after weeks of talks. Meanwhile, unions are staging May Day rallies to demand better work conditions and a wealth tax.

stories.title: Ukraine plans army pay hikes, phased discharge — Zelenskyy
stories.summary: The Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced reforms to boost morale and address military complaints about exhaustion and uneven workloads on the frontline.

stories.title: Iran executes 21-year-old karate champion as UN warns of death penalty surge
stories.summary: Tehran executed 21-year-old karate athlete Sassan Azadvar early Thursday after protest arrest, as UN and rights groups warn of a surge in executions and mass detentions since the start of the Iran war.

stories.title: Iran war impact on shipping affecting refugee aid with freight rates soaring, UNHCR says
stories.summary: The agency has re-routed sea cargo via Jordan's Aqaba Red Sea port and has switched to land corridors including truck routes from Dubai across the Arabian peninsula and Turkey.

stories.title: Jailed Hong Kong pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai wins free speech award in Germany
stories.summary: Media tycoon honoured in absentia as critics decry his 20-year sentence under national security law The jailed media entrepreneur Jimmy Lai has been awarded Deutsche Welle’s freedom of speech award for his contribution to Hong Kong’s pro-democracy movement. The German public broadcaster said on Thursday that Lai would be presented in absentia with the 12th iteration of the award on 23 June at the DW Global Media Forum in Bonn. Continue reading...

stories.title: Shock in India after man takes remains of his sister to bank to prove her death
stories.summary: Jitu Munda says he was refused access to money in case highlighting ‘lack of humanity’ in Indian bureaucracy The sight of a man bringing the remains of his dead sister to a bank in India after officials had refused to let him withdraw money without proof of her death has caused shock in India. Jitu Munda, 52, from the Indian state of Odisha, was captured on video carrying the remains of his recently deceased sister through the streets of Keonjhar and placing them outside the local bank. Continue

stories.title: Georg Baselitz – a life in pictures
stories.summary: The German painter, who has died aged 88 , captured the great rupture of the 20th century, which shaped a body of work that helped redefine postwar art Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: Tornadoes and giant hail in central US
stories.summary: Strongest tornado hits Mineral Wells, Texas, where disaster was declared. Elsewhere, extreme rain inundates China Spring is the season for severe thunderstorms across the central US, and the start of this week was a particularly active period for the region. A favourable weather pattern fuelled intense thunderstorms on Monday through Wednesday, bringing strong winds, very large hail and strong tornadoes. Eight tornadoes were reported on Monday, including an EF2 tornado that ripped through the to

stories.title: CCTV captures moment man attacks nun in Jerusalem – video
stories.summary: Israeli police released a video on Thursday showing an attack on a French Catholic nun in Jerusalem. The footage shows the man running up behind the nun and pushing her to the ground. After briefly walking away, the attacker, who appears to be Jewish, returns to kick the nun as she lies on the ground and only stops when a passerby intervenes. Police said they had arrested a 36-year-old man. The date when the video was recorded was verified by an official statement from the Israeli foreign minist

stories.title: Germany: Merz hails 'historic' health care reform
stories.summary: The Cabinet has greenlit a health care draft law designed to lower insurance rates. The bill heads to parliament next, but doctors' associations and patient protection advocates say it amounts to austerity measures.

stories.title: Russia to hold Victory Day parade without weaponry display
stories.summary: The scaled back parade comes amid increasing Ukrainian attacks deep into mainland Russia, including the capital Moscow. The last time a Victory Day parade was held without weaponry display was in 2007.

stories.title: Teenage bullying increases in Ukrainian schools
stories.summary: The number of teenage victims of bullying is rising in Ukrainian schools. Experts attribute this to war trauma and chronic stress among teenagers.
